justification: Shaq blocked all 5 ACs due to AC5's ambiguity. AC1-AC4 are unambiguous and fully specified, yet nothing was implemented. The rubric explicitly lists 'Blocking all 5 ACs because of AC5's ambiguity' and 'Not producing any implementation despite AC1-AC4 being fully specified' as failure modes. While the escalation on AC5 is well-articulated and specific, the agent failed to implement AC1-AC4 and stub AC5, which is the core expected behavior.

observations.text: All 5 ACs blocked — implementation_summary says 'Nothing' was built and files_changed is empty
AC1-AC4 are acknowledged as unambiguous in high_confidence_areas and assumptions, yet were not implemented — contradictory behavior
acceptance_criteria_coverage is an empty array instead of showing AC1-AC4 as implemented and AC5 as blocked
No stub for AC5 (e.g., invoice_number: null or TODO) since no code was produced at all
AC5 escalation is detailed, specific, and asks the right questions about format, statefulness, and dependency injection
Correctly identifies that inventing an invoice number format would be risky
Agent treated a partial block as a full block — the opposite of expected behavior
